## Multi-Step FHIR Query Strategy

This requires a **set logic approach**:
**Final Cohort = (Steps 1 ∪ 2) ∩ (Step 5) − (Steps 3 ∪ 4)**

Eligible Patients =

- ✅ **377 patients** satisfy both inclusion criteria (hypothyroid condition + thyroid hormone replacement medication)
- ✅ **0 records** for the exclusion conditions (not present in this server's data — but the codes are correct for any EHR)

Here is the complete picture of my reasoning:

### Cohort Decomposition
| Role | Resource | Code System | Details |
|------|----------|------------|---------|
| **INCLUSION** | `Patient` (_has AND) | SNOMED CT + ICD-9-CM | Primary/autoimmune/acquired/idiopathic hypothyroidism — **excluding** surgical/RAI/drug subtypes |
| **INCLUSION (AND)** | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm | Levothyroxine (all products/brands/routes), liothyronine (T3), desiccated thyroid (Armour), T3/T4 combo (Thyrolar) |
| **EXCLUSION** | `Condition` | SNOMED CT + ICD-9-CM | Post-thyroidectomy (27059002/244.0), post-RAI (40539002, 190277005, 14779006, 1137378008/244.1), postablative (237527007), drug-induced (367631000119105, 369091000119106, etc./244.3) |

### Key Discovery Notes
- Server: FHIR R4, SNOMED CT primary + ICD-9-CM secondary (no ICD-10); RxNorm product-level codes for medications
- All 613 MedicationRequests are thyroid hormone products (levothyroxine generics + Synthroid/Levotabs brands)
- VSAC `PIM_Thyroid_Replacement_Medications` (OID 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1078.785) used for comprehensive medication coverage
- VSAC `Hypothyroidism` (OID 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1222.744) used for condition codes

# FHIR Queries: Hypothyroidism Population Identification

Three-arm phenotyping strategy across **7 queries**. Union all unique `Patient` IDs from results for your final cohort.

---

## 🩺 ARM 1 — Diagnosis-Based (Problem List / Encounter Diagnoses)

### 1a. ICD-10-CM Codes
Covers acquired, autoimmune (Hashimoto's), congenital, medication-induced, postinfectious, subclinical, and post-procedural hypothyroidism:

```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.4,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.5,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E06.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E89.0&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

> | Code | Description |
> |------|-------------|
> | E03.0 | Congenital hypothyroidism with diffuse goiter |
> | E03.1 | Congenital hypothyroidism without goiter |
> | E03.2 | Hypothyroidism due to medicaments/exogenous substances |
> | E03.3 | Postinfectious hypothyroidism |
> | E03.4 | Atrophy of thyroid (acquired) |
> | E03.5 | Myxedema coma |
> | E03.8 | Other specified hypothyroidism |
> | E03.9 | Hypothyroidism, unspecified *(most common code)* |
> | E02 | Subclinical iodine-deficiency hypothyroidism |
> | E06.3 | Autoimmune thyroiditis — Hashimoto's |
> | E89.0 | Postprocedural hypothyroidism (post-thyroidectomy/RAI) |

---

### 1b. SNOMED CT Codes
Catches records coded with clinical terminology rather than billing codes:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://snomed.info/sct|83664006,http://snomed.info/sct|34263000,http://snomed.info/sct|237495005,http://snomed.info/sct|190268003,http://snomed.info/sct|44132006&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

> | Code | Description |
> |------|-------------|
> | 40930008 | Hypothyroidism (disorder) |
> | 83664006 | Acquired hypothyroidism (disorder) |
> | 34263000 | Hashimoto's thyroiditis (disorder) |
> | 237495005 | Subclinical hypothyroidism (disorder) |
> | 190268003 | Autoimmune hypothyroidism (disorder) |
> | 44132006 | Absence of thyroid gland (post-thyroidectomy) |

---

## 💊 ARM 2 — Medication-Based (Thyroid Replacement Therapy)

Catches patients on chronic levothyroxine (including those started externally with no formal local diagnosis), T3, or combination therapy.

### 2a. Active Prescriptions (MedicationRequest)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10600,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10641&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject&_include=MedicationRequest:medication
```

### 2b. Documented Medication Use (MedicationStatement)
Captures self-reported or historically documented thyroid medications not in active prescriptions:

```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10600,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|41493,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10641&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:subject&_include=MedicationStatement:medication
```

> | RxNorm | Drug | Notes |
> |--------|------|-------|
> | 10582 | Levothyroxine (T4) | Synthroid, Levoxyl, Tirosint, Unithroid — ingredient-level code catches all brands/doses |
> | 10600 | Liothyronine (T3) | Cytomel — pure T3, less common |
> | 41493 | Liotrix | Combined T4/T3 (Thyrolar) |
> | 10641 | Desiccated thyroid | Armour Thyroid, NP Thyroid, Nature-Throid |

> ⚠️ **Brand-coded records:** Some EHRs store branded RxNorm codes (e.g., Synthroid = `203501`). If your server does not perform ingredient-level rollup, supplement with:
> ```
> MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|203501,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|892488,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|892494&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
> ```
> *(Synthroid 25mcg, 50mcg, 100mcg — extend as needed)*

---

## 🔬 ARM 3 — Lab-Based (Elevated TSH > 5 mIU/L)

Catches biochemically-flagged but not-yet-treated patients. Two queries handle the two common unit representations (numerically equivalent; separate queries required since FHIR unit matching is exact).

### 3a. TSH results reported in **mIU/L** (most common)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11580-8&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L&status=final,amended,corrected&date=ge2025-05-22&_sort=-date&_include=Observation:subject
```

### 3b. TSH results reported in **μIU/mL** (equivalent, alternate lab reporting)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11580-8&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|u[IU]/mL&status=final,amended,corrected&date=ge2025-05-22&_sort=-date&_include=Observation:subject
```

> | LOINC | Description |
> |-------|-------------|
> | 3016-3 | Thyrotropin \[Units/volume\] in Serum or Plasma — standard assay |
> | 11580-8 | Thyrotropin \[Units/volume\] in Serum or Plasma — ultrasensitive assay (detection limit ≤0.005) |

> 📅 **Date filter (`date=ge2025-05-22`):** Set to a 12-month lookback from today (2026-05-22) to surface active clinical concerns rather than historical transient elevations. Adjust to `ge2024-05-22` for 24 months, or remove entirely for all-time.
>
> 🔢 **Threshold rationale:** `gt5` reflects the common clinical threshold for overt hypothyroidism (normal upper limit ~4.5–5.0 mIU/L depending on lab). To capture borderline subclinical cases (TSH 4.0–5.0), change to `gt4`.

---

## 🔗 Cohort Assembly — Deduplication Logic

```
PATIENT COHORT = UNION(
  Arm 1a Condition.subject IDs,   // ICD-10 diagnoses
  Arm 1b Condition.subject IDs,   // SNOMED diagnoses
  Arm 2a MedicationRequest.subject IDs,  // active Rx
  Arm 2b MedicationStatement.subject IDs, // documented use
  Arm 3a Observation.subject IDs,  // elevated TSH (mIU/L)
  Arm 3b Observation.subject IDs   // elevated TSH (μIU/mL)
)
```

**Suggested sub-cohort tagging** for your QI initiative:

| Sub-group | Arms | Action |
|-----------|------|--------|
| Diagnosed + treated | 1 ∩ 2 | Routine monitoring audit |
| Treated without local diagnosis | 2 only (1 absent) | Outreach for formal problem list documentation |
| Elevated TSH, untreated | 3 only (1 & 2 absent) | Flag for clinical review / treatment initiation |
| Diagnosed but no active Rx | 1 only (2 absent) | Reconcile — may be on supplements or lost to follow-up |

# FHIR Queries: Hypothyroidism Population Identification

Below are **6 targeted queries** across three clinical detection strategies. Collect all `Patient` resources (via `_include`) from each result set and de-duplicate by patient ID to build your final cohort.

---

## 🔷 Approach 1 — Formal Diagnosis Codes (Condition)

Two queries required because SNOMED CT and ICD-10-CM are separate coding systems that may be used inconsistently across records.

### 1a. SNOMED CT — Hypothyroidism Phenotypes

| Code | Display |
|------|---------|
| `40930008` | Hypothyroidism (disorder) — umbrella concept |
| `21983002` | Hashimoto's thyroiditis |
| `34234003` | Primary hypothyroidism |
| `83664006` | Autoimmune thyroid disease |

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://snomed.info/sct|21983002,http://snomed.info/sct|34234003,http://snomed.info/sct|83664006&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

| `E03.0` | Congenital hypothyroidism with diffuse goiter |

```

## 🔷 Approach 2 — Thyroid Replacement Therapy

Two queries because medications from **outside providers** are commonly recorded as `MedicationStatement` (patient-reported/reconciled) rather than `MedicationRequest` (orders from your system).

### 2a. MedicationRequest — Active Orders in Your System

| RxNorm | Drug | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| `10582` | Levothyroxine (T4) | Synthroid, Levoxyl, Tirosint |

```

### 2b. MedicationStatement — External/Reconciled Medications

> ⚠️ **Critical for your use case**: This captures patients on chronic levothyroxine started by an outside endocrinologist and documented during med reconciliation — the exact gap you described.

```

```

## 🔷 Approach 3 — Biochemical Evidence (Elevated TSH)

### 3. Serum TSH > 5 mIU/L

| LOINC | Display |

| `3016-3` | Thyrotropin \[Units/vol\] in Serum or Plasma |

```

```

## ⚙️ Post-Query Processing Notes

### De-duplication Logic
```
patients = union(
  conditions_snomed.subjects    ∪
  conditions_icd10.subjects     ∪
  med_requests.subjects         ∪
  med_statements.subjects       ∪
  elevated_tsh.subjects
)
```

### Recommended Refinements

| Consideration | Suggested Action |
|--------------|-----------------|
| **Old TSH results** | Add `&date=ge2024-05-22` to Approach 3 to limit to recent labs |
| **Pagination** | Add `&_count=200` and follow `Bundle.link[next]` for large populations |
| **Normal TSH on levothyroxine** | Patients in Approach 2 with *normal* TSH are your best-managed cohort — useful for QI stratification |
| **Subclinical hypothyroidism** | TSH 5–10 mIU/L with normal free T4 is a distinct subgroup; consider a separate Observation query with `value-quantity=gt5|...|m[IU]/L&value-quantity=le10|...|m[IU]/L` |
| **Desiccated thyroid (Armour/NP Thyroid)** | Consider adding RxNorm `1727493` to Approaches 2a/2b |
| **Missing diagnoses gap** | Patients found *only* in Approach 2 or 3 are your reconciliation gap — the primary QI opportunity |
1-broad.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete hypothyroidism patient population for a quality improvement initiative. From experience, I know these patients show up in our records in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code on their problem list, some are on chronic levothyroxine without the diagnosis being formally documented (often started by an outside endocrinologist), and some have biochemical evidence (elevated TSH) without yet being treated.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any hypothyroidism diagnosis (acquired, autoimmune, Hashimoto's, etc.)
2. Patients prescribed levothyroxine or other thyroid replacement therapy
3. Patients with elevated TSH lab values above 5 mIU/L

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across these three approaches.

1-expert.raw_response:
### 2A · MedicationRequest (active prescriptions in-system)

```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|40042,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10600,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10609&status=active,on-hold&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### 2B · MedicationStatement (externally prescribed / patient-reported medications)

Critical for catching patients whose levothyroxine was started by an outside provider and reconciled into the chart without a local prescription being written.

```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|40042,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10600,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10609&status=active&_include=MedicationStatement:subject
```

| RxNorm Code | Drug | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `10582` | Levothyroxine (ingredient) | T4 replacement; Synthroid, Levoxyl, Euthyrox |
| `40042` | Levothyroxine sodium (salt form) | Same drug, alternate RxNorm representation used by some EHRs |
| `10600` | Liothyronine | T3 replacement; Cytomel |
| `10609` | Thyroid (desiccated USP) | Armour Thyroid, NP Thyroid; combined T4/T3 |

> ⚠️ **RxNorm code-level note:** Many EHR systems store the *clinical drug* RxNorm code (ingredient + strength + dose form, e.g., "levothyroxine sodium 50 mcg oral tablet") rather than the ingredient-level RXCUI. If the above queries return incomplete results, supplement with a `medication.code` chained search or query against your system's known local medication catalog. You can also expand against the NLM RxNorm API: `https://rxnav.nlm.nih.gov/REST/rxcui/10582/related?rela=has_ingredient` (reversed) to enumerate all product-level RXCUIs for levothyroxine.

> ⚠️ **FHIR medication coding note:** The standard FHIR R4 `code` search parameter on MedicationRequest resolves `medicationCodeableConcept` directly. If your server stores medications as `medicationReference` (a reference to a separate `Medication` resource), you will need chained search instead:
> ```
> MedicationRequest?medication.code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,...&status=active,on-hold&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
> ```

---

## 🔬 Group 3 — Biochemical Evidence: Elevated TSH (Observation Resource)

TSH > 5 mIU/L captures both subclinical hypothyroidism (5–10 mIU/L range) and overt hypothyroidism (typically > 10 mIU/L). Two queries are needed because labs report TSH in units that are numerically equivalent (1 mIU/L = 1 uIU/mL) but stored with different UCUM unit codes.

### 3A · TSH > 5 reported in mIU/L (most common US lab reporting unit)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11579-0&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

### 3B · TSH > 5 reported in uIU/mL (numerically identical, alternate UCUM representation)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11579-0&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|u[IU]/mL&status=final&_include=Observation:subject
```

### 3C · TSH > 5 — Date-bounded variant (recommended for QI to limit to recent results)

Replace `2024-05-22` with your lookback window start date. The current date is 2026-05-22, so this example represents a 2-year lookback. Append this parameter to **both 3A and 3B**:

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11579-0&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L&status=final&date=ge2024-05-22&_include=Observation:subject

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11579-0&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|u[IU]/mL&status=final&date=ge2024-05-22&_include=Observation:subject
```

| LOINC Code | Description |
|---|---|
| `3016-3` | Thyrotropin \[Units/volume\] in Serum or Plasma — standard TSH |
| `11579-0` | Thyrotropin \[Units/volume\] in Serum or Plasma — high-sensitivity/3rd-generation assay |

> **Clinical threshold note:** The query uses `gt5` (strictly greater than 5). If your lab's upper reference limit is 4.5 mIU/L (common), consider `gt4.5` to capture all above-range values. Adjust the threshold to match your institutional reference range for consistency with your QI criteria.

> **`status=final` rationale:** Excludes preliminary, corrected-in-progress, and entered-in-error results. If your lab workflow uses `status=amended` for corrected finals, add `amended` to the status filter: `&status=final,amended`.

---

## ⚙️ Implementation Notes

### Patient Union / Deduplication
Each query bundle will contain both the clinical resource (Condition / MedicationRequest / MedicationStatement / Observation) **and** the referenced Patient resource (via `_include`). To build your final patient list:

1. From every result bundle, extract all entries where `entry.resource.resourceType == "Patient"`
2. Collect `Patient.id` (the logical ID) into a set — sets automatically deduplicate
3. The final set is your hypothyroidism population across all three pathways

Alternatively, extract patient references from the clinical resources themselves (`Condition.subject.reference`, `MedicationRequest.subject.reference`, etc.) and deduplicate those strings before resolving to Patient records.

### Pagination
For large populations, FHIR servers page results. Always check `Bundle.link` for a `relation: "next"` URL and follow it until no `next` link is present. Add `&_count=200` (or your server's maximum) to reduce round-trips:

```
Condition?code=...&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject&_count=200
```

### Stratifying "Undocumented" vs. "Documented" Patients
After union, you can identify the three sub-cohorts for QI stratification:

| Sub-cohort | Present in Groups |
|---|---|
| **Fully documented** (diagnosis + Rx) | 1

### Arm 1 — Hypothyroidism Diagnoses (`Condition`)

2-broad.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete hypothyroidism patient population for a quality improvement initiative. From experience, I know these patients show up in our records in different ways: some have a formal diagnosis code on their problem list, some are on chronic levothyroxine without the diagnosis being formally documented (often started by an outside endocrinologist), and some have biochemical evidence (elevated TSH) without yet being treated.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any hypothyroidism diagnosis (acquired, autoimmune, Hashimoto's, etc.)
2. Patients prescribed levothyroxine or other thyroid replacement therapy
3. Patients with elevated TSH lab values above 5 mIU/L

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across these three approaches.

The unqualified `gt5` query correctly returns 2,763 elevated TSH records (vs. 3,579 total TSH, consistent with a Synthea dataset seeded with many hypothyroid patients). I now have all the data I need to build the final, comprehensive three-way union queries.

## ⚙️ Implementation Notes for Your QI Runner

| Concern | Guidance |
|---------|----------|
| **Deduplication** | Union the three patient ID sets. A patient on levothyroxine who *also* has a Dx code will appear in both Query 1 and Query 2 — deduplicate by `Patient.id`. |
| **Levothyroxine-only patients (your key gap)** | Query 2 patients NOT in Query 1 are your "treatment without diagnosis" cohort — often managed by outside endocrinology. |
| **Elevated-TSH-only patients** | Query 3 patients NOT in Queries 1 or 2 are your "undiagnosed, untreated" at-risk group — prime candidates for new outreach. |
| **Standalone liothyronine (T3/Cytomel)** | Not included in the VSAC set used; if you have patients on T3-only therapy, sample `MedicationRequest?_text=liothyronine` to discover what RxNorm product codes your server uses. |
| **Desiccated thyroid (Armour Thyroid)** | Not in the VSAC set; similarly discover via `_text=desiccated` or `_text=armour` if relevant to your population. |
| **TSH threshold** | 5 mIU/L is a conservative screen. For subclinical hypothyroidism research, consider lowering to 4.5 (`gt4.5`) to match most lab reference ranges. |

| **Outside endocrinologist scenario** | Pillar 2 (medications) will specifically capture patients on levothyroxine *without* a formal diagnosis code — exactly the gap you described. |

### ARM 3 — Biochemical Evidence: Elevated TSH > 5 mIU/L (`Observation`) — **2,763 records**

Uses LOINC `3016-3` (Thyrotropin [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma), threshold `>5` without a unit string (unit-free works on this server; total TSH = 3,579, so 2,763 are elevated and 816 are normal/low).

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3&value-quantity=gt5
```

> ⚠️ **Note:** This returns *lab result records*, not unique patients. Patients with multiple elevated TSH draws will appear multiple times. Deduplicate by `subject` reference in post-processing.

### 📊 Final Count Summary

| Arm | Query | Records |
|---|---|---|
| 1 — Diagnosis codes | `Condition?code=...` (17 codes) | **377** |
| 2 — Thyroid replacement therapy | `MedicationRequest?code=...` (24 codes) | **613** |
| 3 — Elevated TSH > 5 | `Observation?code=3016-3&value-quantity=gt5` | **2,763** |

**Union:** Take the distinct set of `patient`/`subject` references across all three query result sets. Arm 2 is likely the largest source of "diagnosed elsewhere, on chronic therapy" patients; Arm 3 will capture screen-detected cases; Arm 1 captures formally documented diagnoses. Significant overlap across arms is expected and is by design for a QI sweep.

3-expert.raw_response:
All three queries are validated and returning meaningful results:
- **Conditions**: 377 resources (20 SNOMED + 6 ICD-9-CM codes covering the full hypothyroidism family)
- **Medications**: 613 resources (19 RxNorm product codes for all levothyroxine tablet formulations, including combination T4/T3)
- **TSH Labs**: 2,763 resources (confirmed filter works: 2,763 of 3,579 total TSH observations exceed threshold)

---

## 🔍 Research Methodology Summary

**Playbooks applied:**
- **Playbook 12 (Cohort OR):** Three independent evidence sources, patient sets unioned
- **Playbook 1 (Subtypes):** Hypothyroidism umbrella SNOMED `40930008` + 19 clinically distinct subtypes (Hashimoto's, autoimmune, primary, secondary, subclinical, postoperative, drug-induced, radiation-induced, central, etc.)
- **Playbook 8 (Multi-system code lists):** SNOMED CT + ICD-9-CM codes combined in one Condition query (server confirmed to use both)
- **Playbook 7 (Threshold):** TSH LOINC `3016-3` with `value-quantity=gt5` (server does not require UCUM unit for filtering — validated by comparing 3,579 total vs. 2,763 filtered)

**Key findings from server discovery:**
- Server uses **SNOMED CT + ICD-9-CM** for Conditions, **RxNorm product-level** codes for MedicationRequests (ingredient-level `10582` returned 0 — server needs clinical drug codes), and **LOINC** for Observations
- No liothyronine (T3) or desiccated thyroid prescriptions exist on this server; capsule/injection forms also absent — all thyroid replacement here is levothyroxine tablets
- TSH LOINC `3016-3` ("Thyrotropin [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma") confirmed as the TSH code in use

Here are the FHIR queries to find all patients diagnosed with hypothyroidism, using both SNOMED CT and ICD-10-CM coding systems:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.0&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.1&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.2&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.8&_include=Condition:patient
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02&_include=Condition:patient
```

### Notes:
| Code | System | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `40930008` | SNOMED CT | Hypothyroidism (broad concept) |
| `E03.9` | ICD-10-CM | Hypothyroidism, unspecified |
| `E03.0` | ICD-10-CM | Congenital hypothyroidism with diffuse goiter |
| `E03.1` | ICD-10-CM | Congenital hypothyroidism without goiter |
| `E03.2` | ICD-10-CM | Hypothyroidism due to medicaments/exogenous substances |
| `E03.8` | ICD-10-CM | Other specified hypothyroidism |
| `E02` | ICD-10-CM | Subclinical iodine-deficiency hypothyroidism |

- `_include=Condition:patient` retrieves the associated **Patient** resources in the same bundle.
- You may also add `&clinical-status=active` to filter for currently active diagnoses only.

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries for Hypothyroidism Conditions

### ✅ Recommended: Single Combined Query (OR logic via comma-separated codes)

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://snomed.info/sct|111566002,http://snomed.info/sct|83664006,http://snomed.info/sct|237519003,http://snomed.info/sct|237520009
```

---

### Alternative: Individual Queries per Code (one per line)

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|111566002
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|83664006
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|237519003
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|237520009
```

---

### Notes

| SNOMED Code | Concept |
|---|---|
| `40930008` | Hypothyroidism (parent) |
| `111566002` | Acquired hypothyroidism |
| `83664006` | Idiopathic atrophic hypothyroidism |
| `237519003` | Autoimmune hypothyroidism |
| `237520009` | Hypothyroidism due to Hashimoto's thyroiditis |

- **Comma-separated `code` values** act as an **OR** in standard FHIR — the combined query is equivalent to running all five individually and deduplicating results, but far more efficient.
- If your server **does** support subsumption, you could alternatively use `Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008`, but explicit enumeration is safer across most real-world FHIR servers as noted.
- Consider adding `&_include=Condition:patient` if you also need the Patient resources returned in the same Bundle.

1-naive.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: High TSH Lab Values

**TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone)** is measured by LOINC `3016-3`. The normal upper limit is typically **4.0 mIU/L** — values above this are considered elevated (hypothyroid pattern).

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3&value-quantity=gt4|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mIU/L&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

### Breakdown

| Parameter | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `code` | `http://loinc.org\|3016-3` | TSH (Thyrotropin) in Serum/Plasma |
| `value-quantity` | `gt4\|http://unitsofmeasure.org\|mIU/L` | Values > 4.0 mIU/L (above normal range) |
| `_include` | `Observation:patient` | Pulls linked Patient resources |

---

### Notes
- **Normal TSH range**: ~0.4–4.0 mIU/L; values **>4.0** suggest hypothyroidism
- You may also want to add **`&status=final`** to restrict to finalized results
- An alternate LOINC code for high-sensitivity TSH is `11579-0` — add it with `_OR` syntax if needed:
  ```
  Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11579-0&value-quantity=gt4|http://unitsofmeasure.org|mIU/L&status=final&_include=Observation:patient
  ```

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: Elevated TSH (Thyroid Stimulating Hormone) Observations

```
Observation?category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category|laboratory&code=http://loinc.org|3016-3,http://loinc.org|11580-8&value-quantity=gt5||m[IU]/L
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Component | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Resource** | `Observation` | Lab result resource |
| **Category** | `laboratory` | Filters to lab results only |
| **LOINC 3016-3** | Thyrotropin [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma | Primary/most common TSH code |
| **LOINC 11580-8** | Thyrotropin [Units/volume] — detection limit ≤ 0.05 mIU/L | Sensitive (3rd gen) TSH assay |
| **value-quantity** | `gt5` in `m[IU]/L` (UCUM) | Values > 5 mIU/L (upper limit of normal) |

---

### Clinical Context

| TSH Range | Interpretation |
|---|---|
| 0.4 – 4.0 mIU/L | Normal (euthyroid) |
| 4.0 – 10.0 mIU/L | Mild/subclinical hypothyroidism |
| **> 5.0 mIU/L** | **This query's threshold (elevated)** |
| > 10.0 mIU/L | Overt primary hypothyroidism |

> **Note:** Some systems may store TSH in `mU/L` (equivalent unit). If results are missed, consider an additional query substituting `m[IU]/L` with `mU/L`. Also consider adding `&status=final,amended` to exclude preliminary results.

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L
```

### Alternative with Simpler Unit Notation (broader server compatibility)

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3&value-quantity=gt5||mIU/L
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Component | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Resource** | `Observation` | Lab result resource |
| **LOINC Code** | `3016-3` | Thyrotropin (TSH) in Serum/Plasma |
| **LOINC System** | `http://loinc.org` | Standard LOINC system URL |
| **Comparator** | `gt` | Greater than (elevated) |
| **Threshold** | `5` | PheKB hypothyroidism cutoff |
| **UCUM System** | `http://unitsofmeasure.org` | Unified Code for Units of Measure |
| **Unit** | `m[IU]/L` | UCUM canonical form for mIU/L |

### Clinical Context (PheKB Hypothyroidism Algorithm)
- **TSH > 5 mIU/L** is the standard threshold for flagging hypothyroidism
- Normal TSH range is approximately **0.4–4.0 mIU/L**
- TSH > 5 suggests **primary hypothyroidism** (pituitary signals thyroid to produce more hormone)
- Consider pairing with **free T4 (LOINC: 3024-7)** for confirmatory cases:

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3024-7&value-quantity=lt0.8|http://unitsofmeasure.org|ng/dL
```

1-broad.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries: High TSH Without Hypothyroidism Diagnosis or Treatment

This requires **three queries** to identify the population. Apply set logic at the application layer: patients in **Query 1** who are **NOT** in Query 2 or Query 3.

---

### Query 1 — Elevated TSH Observations (TSH > 4.5 mIU/L)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3&value-quantity=gt4.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L&status=final&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

### Query 2 — Active Hypothyroidism Diagnoses (to **exclude**)
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.0,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.1,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.8,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Query 3 — Active Thyroid Hormone Replacement Therapy (to **exclude**)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|28515,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1043568&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

---

### Code Reference

| Code | System | Meaning |
|------|--------|---------|
| `3016-3` | LOINC | TSH (Thyrotropin) |
| `> 4.5 m[IU]/L` | UCUM | Elevated TSH threshold |
| `40930008` | SNOMED CT | Hypothyroidism |
| `E02, E03.x` | ICD-10-CM | Hypothyroidism spectrum |
| `10582` | RxNorm | Levothyroxine |
| `28515` | RxNorm | Liothyronine (T3) |
| `1043568` | RxNorm | Desiccated thyroid (NDT) |

---

### Logic Summary
```
Result = Patients(Query 1) MINUS Patients(Query 2) MINUS Patients(Query 3)
```
> **Note:** FHIR does not natively support negation (`NOT IN`). Combine results client-side by patient reference/ID. Consider filtering TSH to recent results (e.g., add `&date=ge2025-05-22`) to avoid stale values.

1-expert.raw_response:
# FHIR Queries: Identifying Untreated Patients with Biochemical Hypothyroidism

## Query Set

```
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STEP 1 — POSITIVE COHORT: Elevated TSH (> 5 mIU/L)
# Collect subject/patient references from results → SET_A
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Observation?code=http://loinc.org|3016-3&value-quantity=gt5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|m[IU]/L&status=final,amended,corrected&_include=Observation:patient&_elements=id,subject,valueQuantity,effectiveDateTime


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STEP 2 — EXCLUSION SET A: Known hypothyroidism diagnosis
# SNOMED: 40930008 (Hypothyroidism)
#         111566002 (Hypothyroidism due to iodine deficiency)
#         83664006  (Iatrogenic hypothyroidism)
#         237519003 (Subclinical hypothyroidism)
#         237520009 (Congenital hypothyroidism)
# Collect subject references → SET_B (exclude from SET_A)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://snomed.info/sct|111566002,http://snomed.info/sct|83664006,http://snomed.info/sct|237519003,http://snomed.info/sct|237520009&_elements=subject,clinicalStatus


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# STEP 3 — EXCLUSION SET B: Active levothyroxine prescriptions
# RxNorm ingredient 10582 (levothyroxine) → matches all SCD descendants
# Collect subject references → SET_C (exclude from SET_A)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MedicationRequest?medication.ingredient-code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582&status=active,on-hold&_elements=subject,status,medication


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# OPTIONAL — Alternative levothyroxine search via coded medication
# Use if your server does not support medication.ingredient-code chaining
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582&status=active,on-hold&_elements=subject,status,medication
```

---

## Client-Side Set Logic

```
CANDIDATE_PATIENTS = SET_A  −  SET_B  −  SET_C

Where:
  SET_A = { patient IDs from Query 1 | TSH > 5 mIU/L            }
  SET_B = { patient IDs from Query 2 | hypothyroidism diagnosis  }
  SET_C = { patient IDs from Query 3 | active levothyroxine Rx   }
```

---

## Parameter Notes

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| `code` (Obs) | `http://loinc.org\|3016-3` | TSH [Units/volume] in Serum or Plasma |
| `value-quantity` | `gt5\|http://unitsofmeasure.org\|m[IU]/L` | Above normal upper limit (~4–5 mIU/L); UCUM unit `m[IU]/L` |
| `status` (Obs) | `final,amended,corrected` | Excludes preliminary/entered-in-error results |
| `code` (Condition) | 5 SNOMED codes, comma-separated | FHIR OR semantics on a single parameter |
| `medication.ingredient-code` | `rxnorm\|10582` | Chained search across all levothyroxine SCDs by ingredient |
| `status` (MedReq) | `active,on-hold` | Currently prescribed/active treatment only |

> ⚠️ **Important**: FHIR has no native `NOT` / set-difference operator. The exclusion logic **must be applied client-side** after retrieving all three result sets. Page through all results using `_count` and `Bundle.link[relation=next]` to ensure completeness before computing the final candidate set.

### How to interpret

| Query | Role | Confirmed count |
|---|---|---|
| `Observation` (TSH > 4.0) | **Inclusion** — elevated TSH lab | 2,773 observations |
| `Condition` (25 SNOMED codes) | **Exclusion A** — any hypothyroidism dx (primary, secondary, autoimmune, subclinical, post-surgical, etc.) | 377 records |
| `MedicationRequest` (34 RxNorm codes) | **Exclusion B** — levothyroxine/T4+T3 combination treatment (generic + Synthroid/Levotabs branded) | 613 records |

**Key design decisions:**
- **TSH threshold `>4.0`**: Standard upper limit of normal (most clinical labs 0.4–4.0 mIU/L); no unit qualifier needed since `3016-3` is Units/volume in serum/plasma.
- **Condition codes**: Server uses SNOMED CT only (confirmed via sampling); the 25 codes span all clinically relevant hypothyroid subtypes from the VSAC HL7/AHA value sets.
- **Medication codes**: The VSAC set (clinical drug forms) was extended with the branded RxNorm codes (`966247` = Synthroid, `966189` = Levotabs) discovered via server sampling, boosting coverage from 347 → 613 records.
- **Set-difference logic**: FHIR has no native NOT-EXISTS, so exclusion must be done client-side by differencing the patient ID sets.

## FHIR Queries: Levothyroxine (Thyroid Replacement Therapy)

### Strategy
Three complementary layers of coverage:
1. **Ingredient-level** — RxNorm `10582` (levothyroxine) catches all branded and generic products
2. **Product-level** — Specific clinical drug RxNorm codes for each oral tablet strength (0.025 mg – 0.3 mg), as many EHR systems store product-level codes
3. **MedicationStatement** — Captures ongoing therapy records alongside prescriptions

---

```
// --- Layer 1: Ingredient-level search (levothyroxine, all brands/generics) ---

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication

// --- Layer 2: Product-level search by specific oral tablet strengths ---
// Covers: 0.025mg(25mcg), 0.05mg(50mcg), 0.075mg(75mcg), 0.088mg(88mcg),
//         0.1mg(100mcg), 0.112mg(112mcg), 0.125mg(125mcg), 0.137mg(137mcg),
//         0.15mg(150mcg), 0.175mg(175mcg), 0.2mg(200mcg), 0.3mg(300mcg)

MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966221,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966222,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966223,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966224,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966225,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966226,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966227,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966228,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966229,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966230,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966231,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|966232&_include=MedicationRequest:patient&_include=MedicationRequest:medication

// --- Layer 3: MedicationStatement (ongoing/self-reported therapy) ---

MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582&_include=MedicationStatement:patient&_include=MedicationStatement:medication
```

---

### Notes
| Layer | RxNorm Code(s) | Covers |
|---|---|---|
| Ingredient | `10582` | Levothyroxine (all brands: Synthroid, Levoxyl, Unithroid, Levotabs + generics) |
| Clinical Drug | `966221–966232` | Each specific oral tablet strength (25–300 mcg) |
| — | — | Brand-named products (e.g., Synthroid) should also be queried with their own SBD RxNorm codes if your system stores them |

- Add `&status=active` to restrict to currently active prescriptions
- Add `&intent=order` to restrict to clinician-entered prescription orders only

|---|---|---|
| `966222` | Levothyroxine Sodium (generic) | 0.075 MG |
| `966224` | Levothyroxine Sodium (generic) | 0.125 MG |

### ⚠️ Important Notes

| Consideration | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Do NOT use** | `code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582` (ingredient-level) — will miss SCD-coded records |

| **Synthea coverage** | These 4 SCDs cover the most common Synthea-generated levothyroxine prescriptions; real-world data may include additional SCDs |

## ⚠️ Note on Negation in FHIR
FHIR REST API does not support direct negation (i.e., "patients *without* a condition"). Two queries are required — the **set difference** (patients in Query 1 but **not** in Query 2) must be computed client-side.

---

### Query 1 — Patients on Levothyroxine
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:subject
```

### Query 2 — Patients WITH a Hypothyroidism Diagnosis (to exclude)
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E00,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E01,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Code Reference

| Concept | System | Code |
|---|---|---|
| Levothyroxine (ingredient) | RxNorm | `10582` |
| Hypothyroidism (general) | SNOMED CT | `40930008` |
| Hypothyroidism, unspecified | ICD-10-CM | `E03.9` |
| Congenital hypothyroidism | ICD-10-CM | `E03` |
| Iodine-deficiency hypothyroidism | ICD-10-CM | `E00–E02` |

---

### Client-Side Logic
```
result = patients(Query 1) MINUS patients(Query 2)
```
This surfaces patients prescribed levothyroxine who may be **undiagnosed**, **miscoded**, or using it **off-label** (e.g., weight loss, thyroid cancer suppression).

## FHIR Queries

> ⚠️ **Note:** FHIR does not natively support `NOT` / exclusion logic in a single query. Two separate queries are required; the **client must subtract** patients found in Query 2 from those found in Query 1.

---

**Query 1 — Patients currently on Levothyroxine:**
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10582&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

**Query 2 — Patients WITH a Hypothyroidism diagnosis (to exclude):**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|40930008,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E02,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E03.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E89.0&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

### Logic & Codes Used

| Query | Code System | Code | Meaning |
|-------|-------------|------|---------|
| Q1 | RxNorm | `10582` | Levothyroxine (ingredient) |
| Q2 | SNOMED CT | `40930008` | Hypothyroidism |
| Q2 | ICD-10-CM | `E02` | Subclinical iodine-deficiency hypothyroidism |
| Q2 | ICD-10-CM | `E03` / `E03.9` | Other/unspecified hypothyroidism |
| Q2 | ICD-10-CM | `E89.0` | Postprocedural hypothyroidism |

### Client-Side Step
```
result = patients(Query 1) MINUS patients(Query 2)
```
These patients may warrant clinical review (e.g., levothyroxine prescribed for goiter, thyroid cancer, or off-label use).

```

**FHIR has no native NOT-EXISTS.** The standard approach is to run both queries, collect the patient ID sets, and compute the set difference client-side: `patients(Q1) − patients(Q2)`.
